title: Boat Rental in Broome: Costs, Seasons and What to Know
html:
Boat rental in Broome puts the turquoise Kimberley coast within easy reach, with most charters leaving from the Broome Boat Ramp near Town Beach and Roebuck Bay. Through BoatHire24 a shared seat starts near EUR 60, while a private day afloat typically runs from EUR 350 in the high season.

Where to set off in Broome

Broome sits on the Indian Ocean edge of Western Australia, and its launch points reward a little planning around the famously large tides. Most skippers favour calm-water departures and reef-fringed anchorages that stay sheltered through the day.

  • Broome Boat Ramp, the main trailer and tender access beside Town Beach.
  • Roebuck Bay, a wide, shallow bay famous for its bird life and red-cliff backdrop.
  • Gantheaume Point, where red rock meets white sand at the southern end of Cable Beach.
  • Cable Beach, the long west-facing strand best known for sunsets over the ocean.
  • Willie Creek, a tidal inlet north of town near the pearl farms.
  • Entrance Point, close to the old jetty and the deepwater channel into the port.

Which boat to rent in Broome?

Choose by group size and tide: a nimble speedboat suits two to six guests, while a crewed yacht carries a larger party in comfort. Broome rewards craft that can read its big tides and shallow flats, so local operators lean towards stable, shallow-draught hulls. For an agile coastal run, compare a centre-console day boat such as the one on the Limpopo charter page, which handles the Roebuck Bay chop with ease.

  1. Speedboat, best for reef hopping and quick coastal runs, seats up to 6 guests, from EUR 350 per day.
  2. Centre-console day boat, ideal for fishing and snorkelling stops, seats up to 8 guests, from EUR 420 per day.
  3. Sailing catamaran, suited to relaxed full-day cruising, seats up to 12 guests, from EUR 900 per day.
  4. Motor cruiser, comfortable for families wanting shade and a galley, seats up to 10 guests, from EUR 750 per day.
  5. Crewed yacht, the premium choice for sunset charters, seats up to 12 guests, from EUR 1,400 per day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Broome?

Prices in Broome shift with the season and the size of the boat, but the bands below give a reliable starting point in euros for 2026 planning.

  • A shared sunset or wildlife tour seat starts at around EUR 60 per person.
  • A small motorboat for a half-day typically costs from EUR 220.
  • A catamaran for a full day runs from EUR 900.
  • A crewed yacht with skipper begins near EUR 1,400 per day before fuel and extras.

When you are weighing options, it helps to read a yacht charter in Broome listing closely, because quoted rates may exclude fuel, the skipper, or a cleaning fee that the operator confirms before you book.

When is the best season to rent a boat in Broome?

The dry season from May to October offers the calmest, clearest days and is when most charters operate. Broome runs on a tropical wet-and-dry pattern rather than a European summer, so timing matters more than in the Mediterranean. The mornings are usually still, with a steady sea breeze building through the afternoon.

In Broome the afternoon south-westerly sea breeze, known locally as "The Doctor", often freshens after midday in the dry season, so book morning departures for the flattest water and the best visibility over the reefs.

How does booking a boat in Broome work?

You pick your dates, message the operator, and confirm the boat, crew and meeting point before any money changes hands. Western Australia requires a recreational skipper's ticket to drive most powered craft yourself, so many visitors choose a crewed option and let a licensed local handle the helm. The operator confirms the exact licence and safety requirements for each boat, and a clear guide to how boat rental works walks you through deposits, cancellation windows and what to bring on the day.

At a glance

  • Price band: EUR 60 per seat to EUR 1,400 per day
  • Peak months: May to October
  • Trip length: half-day to full-day charters
  • Capacity: 2 to 12 guests on most boats
  • Currency: EUR
  • Nearest highlight: Willie Creek, around 12 nautical miles north

Questions about boat rental in Broome

Do I need a licence to rent a boat in Broome?

Western Australia generally requires a recreational skipper's ticket to operate a powered vessel yourself, so many visitors book a crewed charter instead. The operator confirms the exact licence rules for each boat before you go.

Can I see dolphins or whales on a Broome charter?

Roebuck Bay and the waters off Cable Beach are known for dolphins year-round, and humpback whales pass the Kimberley coast between roughly June and September. A morning departure usually gives the calmest viewing conditions.

How many people can join a charter in Broome?

Most boats carry between 2 and 12 guests, depending on the hull and the operator's licence. Larger crewed yachts can host bigger celebrations, so always confirm the maximum capacity when you request your dates.

What should I bring for a day on the water?

Pack reef-safe sun cream, a hat, water and a light layer for the afternoon breeze, plus any snorkelling gear you prefer. The operator confirms what is provided, including shade, drinks and safety equipment, when you book.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Broome or list your own, and a few minutes of comparing crews and dates usually finds the right day afloat. Browse the BoatHire24 marketplace to check availability across the Kimberley coast and beyond for 2026.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Broome and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
